author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
verses: hari hari! kata dine hena daśā haba śrī⋅rāsa⋅mañjarī saṅge śrī⋅maṇi⋅mañjarī rāṅge śrī⋅rūpera anugā haiba suśītala vṛndāvana ratnavedī suśobhana tāhe maṇimaya siŋhāsana hema nīla⋅kāntidhara rāi⋅kānu sundara tāhe basāiba duijana sakhīra ādeśa habe cāmara ḍhulāba kabe tāmbula jogāba cāṅda⋅mukhe ānandita ha’ba sadā śuddha⋅bhāve prema⋅kathā duhāra pīriti rasa⋅sukhe mallikā mālatī jūthī nānā phule mālā gā̐thi parāiba duhāra galāye rasera ālasa kāle basiyā caraṇa tale sevana kariba duhāra pāye rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa prāṇapati jīvane maraṇe gati ihā vine āra nāhi mane śrī⋅kṛṣṇa⋅caitanya⋅prāṇa svarūpa⋅rūpa⋅sanātana narottama ei nivedana
en_translation:
O Lord Hari! When will I achieve such a state? In the company of Śrī Rāsa-mañjarī and Śrī Maṇi-mañjarī, I will become a follower of Śrīla Rūpa Gosvāmī.

In the very cool and beautiful Vṛndāvana, on a beautiful, jewel-studded altar, I will see a jewel-studded throne. Upon that throne, I will seat the beautiful Divine Couple, who have golden and dark-blue complexions.

When will I receive the order of the gopī friends to fan Them with a whisk? When will I offer betel nuts to Their moon-like faces? I will always be joyful, purely devoted to topics of divine love and the happiness of Their mutual affection.

Stringing garlands of various jasmine flowers, I will place them around Their necks. When They are resting, I will sit at Their lotus feet and render service to both of Them.

Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa are the Lords of my life, my only shelter in life and in death. Without Them, nothing else remains in my mind. Svarūpa Dāmodara, Rūpa Gosvāmī, and Sanātana Gosvāmī are the very life of Lord Caitanya. This is the humble prayer of Narottama Dāsa.
